du culte).jpg|250px]]
Worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=230}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434}}{{ill|lt=Toyokawa City|Q389495|WD=Q389495}} Kosakai-chō, Aza Miyawaki 2-1 |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|47|50.5|N|137|21|56.9|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Utari Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = Unakami Sukune no Mikoto |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (Minor)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|UNKNOWN}} |Founded = (Traditionally) {{ill|lt=Hakuhō period|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612}} year 15 ({{ill|lt=686|Q30619|WD=Q30619}}) |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage status = |Annual festival = Second {{ill|lt=Saturday|Q131|WD=Q131|lt=Sat}} and {{ill|lt=Sunday|Q132|WD=Q132}} of {{ill|lt=April|Q118|WD=Q118}} |Rituals = Wind Festival, Rice Paddy Festival |Map = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Utari Shrine|position=bottom|width=180|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Utari Shrine''' (うたりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Kosakai-chō, Aza Miyawaki, {{ill|lt=Toyokawa City|Q389495|WD=Q389495}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}. Under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking}}, it was designated as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|UNKNOWN}}. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines the following single deity: * Unakami Sukune no Mikoto (うなかみすくねのみこと) === History === The enshrined deity, Unakami Sukune no Mikoto, is said to be a descendant of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōgen|Q333572|WD=Q333572}}, the great-great-grandchild (fourth generation) of {{ill|lt=Katsuragi Sotsuhiko no Mikoto|Q11620922|WD=Q11620922}}. During the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235|WD=Q357235}}, he was appointed as {{ill|lt=Hono no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11597049|WD=Q11597049|lt=provincial governor of Hono no Kuni (present-day Higashi-Mikawa)}}. Katsuragi Sotsuhiko no Mikoto was the father of {{ill|lt=Iwa no Hime no Mikoto|Q1066593|WD=Q1066593}}, the empress of {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119}}, and is said to have wielded great power as a {{ill|lt=local ruling clan|Q84066|WD=Q84066}} of Katsuragi in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}}. After his death, Unakami Sukune no Mikoto was enshrined at Kashiwagi Beach in Hirai, {{ill|lt=Mikawa Province|Q768158|WD=Q768158}}, in recognition of his outstanding achievements in promoting industry and governing the people. It is said that during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181}}, in the 15th year of the {{ill|lt=Hakuhō period|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612}} ({{ill|lt=686|Q30619|WD=Q30619}}), on {{ill|lt=April 11|Q2501|WD=Q2501}}, the shrine was relocated to its present site by Hata no Ishikatsu (はたのいしかつ). In the 6th year of the {{ill|lt=Jōgan era (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635}} ({{ill|lt=864|Q25041|WD=Q25041}}), the shrine’s {{ill|lt=divine rank|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025}} was promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and its name appears in the Jinmyōchō of the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}. During the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707}}, when the Emperor made his eastern progress, the shrine received an imperial envoy. In the 11th year of the {{ill|lt=Meiji era|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} ({{ill|lt=1878|Q7776|WD=Q7776}}), it received a shrine name scroll in the imperial handwriting of {{ill|lt=Prince Arisugawa Taruhito|Q668338|WD=Q668338}}. In the 7th year of the {{ill|lt=Taishō era|Q329174|WD=Q329174}} ({{ill|lt=1918|Q2094|WD=Q2094}}), it was elevated from a village shrine to a prefectural shrine. === Festivals === * Wind Festival (Kazematsuri) – Held every year on the second Saturday and Sunday of April. {{ill|lt=Tezutsu fireworks|Q11497105|WD=Q11497105}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=launched fireworks|UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill|lt=festival floats|Q11470974|WD=Q11470974}} are dedicated. This festival is the origin of the name {{ill|lt=Kozakai Aoi Fūkan|Q108765371|WD=Q108765371}}. * Rice Paddy Festival – Held on the 7th day of the 1st month of the lunar calendar. Worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=230}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434}}{{ill|lt=Toyokawa City|Q389495|WD=Q389495}} Kosakai-chō, Aza Miyawaki 2-1 |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|47|50.5|N|137|21|56.9|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Utari Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = Unakami Sukune no Mikoto |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (Minor)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |Founded = (Traditionally) {{ill|lt=Hakuhō period|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612}} year 15 ({{ill|lt=686|Q30619|WD=Q30619}}) |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage status = |Annual festival = Second {{ill|lt=Saturday|Q131|WD=Q131|lt=Sat}} and {{ill|lt=Sunday|Q132|WD=Q132}} of {{ill|lt=April|Q118|WD=Q118}} |Rituals = Wind Festival, Rice Paddy Festival |Map = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Utari Shrine|position=bottom|width=180|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Utari Shrine''' (うたりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Kosakai-chō, Aza Miyawaki, {{ill|lt=Toyokawa City|Q389495|WD=Q389495}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}. Under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking}}, it was designated as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines the following single deity: * Unakami Sukune no Mikoto (うなかみすくねのみこと) === History === The enshrined deity, Unakami Sukune no Mikoto, is said to be a descendant of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōgen|Q333572|WD=Q333572}}, the great-great-grandchild (fourth generation) of {{ill|lt=Katsuragi Sotsuhiko no Mikoto|Q11620922|WD=Q11620922}}. During the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235|WD=Q357235}}, he was appointed as {{ill|lt=Hono no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11597049|WD=Q11597049|lt=provincial governor of Hono no Kuni (present-day Higashi-Mikawa)}}. Katsuragi Sotsuhiko no Mikoto was the father of {{ill|lt=Iwa no Hime no Mikoto|Q1066593|WD=Q1066593}}, the empress of {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119}}, and is said to have wielded great power as a {{ill|lt=local ruling clan|Q84066|WD=Q84066}} of Katsuragi in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}}. After his death, Unakami Sukune no Mikoto was enshrined at Kashiwagi Beach in Hirai, {{ill|lt=Mikawa Province|Q768158|WD=Q768158}}, in recognition of his outstanding achievements in promoting industry and governing the people. It is said that during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181}}, in the 15th year of the {{ill|lt=Hakuhō period|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612}} ({{ill|lt=686|Q30619|WD=Q30619}}), on {{ill|lt=April 11|Q2501|WD=Q2501}}, the shrine was relocated to its present site by Hata no Ishikatsu (はたのいしかつ). In the 6th year of the {{ill|lt=Jōgan era (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635}} ({{ill|lt=864|Q25041|WD=Q25041}}), the shrine’s {{ill|lt=divine rank|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025}} was promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and its name appears in the Jinmyōchō of the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}. During the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707}}, when the Emperor made his eastern progress, the shrine received an imperial envoy. In the 11th year of the {{ill|lt=Meiji era|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} ({{ill|lt=1878|Q7776|WD=Q7776}}), it received a shrine name scroll in the imperial handwriting of {{ill|lt=Prince Arisugawa Taruhito|Q668338|WD=Q668338}}. In the 7th year of the {{ill|lt=Taishō era|Q329174|WD=Q329174}} ({{ill|lt=1918|Q2094|WD=Q2094}}), it was elevated from a village shrine to a prefectural shrine. === Festivals === * Wind Festival (Kazematsuri) – Held every year on the second Saturday and Sunday of April. {{ill|lt=Tezutsu fireworks|Q11497105|WD=Q11497105}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=launched fireworks|1=UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill|lt=festival floats|Q11470974|WD=Q11470974}} are dedicated. This festival is the origin of the name {{ill|lt=Kozakai Aoi Fūkan|Q108765371|WD=Q108765371}}. * Rice Paddy Festival – Held on the 7th day of the 1st month of the lunar calendar. du culte).jpg|250px]]
Worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=230}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434}}{{ill|lt=Toyokawa City|Q389495|WD=Q389495}} Kosakai-chō, Aza Miyawaki 2-1 |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|47|50.5|N|137|21|56.9|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Utari Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = Unakami Sukune no Mikoto |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (Minor)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |Founded = (Traditionally) {{ill|lt=Hakuhō period|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612}} year 15 ({{ill|lt=686|Q30619|WD=Q30619}}) |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage status = |Annual festival = Second {{ill|lt=Saturday|Q131|WD=Q131|lt=Sat}} and {{ill|lt=Sunday|Q132|WD=Q132}} of {{ill|lt=April|Q118|WD=Q118}} |Rituals = Wind Festival, Rice Paddy Festival |Map = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Utari Shrine|position=bottom|width=180|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Utari Shrine''' (うたりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Kosakai-chō, Aza Miyawaki, {{ill|lt=Toyokawa City|Q389495|WD=Q389495}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}. Under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking}}, it was designated as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines the following single deity: * Unakami Sukune no Mikoto (うなかみすくねのみこと) === History === The enshrined deity, Unakami Sukune no Mikoto, is said to be a descendant of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōgen|Q333572|WD=Q333572}}, the great-great-grandchild (fourth generation) of {{ill|lt=Katsuragi Sotsuhiko no Mikoto|Q11620922|WD=Q11620922}}. During the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235|WD=Q357235}}, he was appointed as {{ill|lt=Hono no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11597049|WD=Q11597049|lt=provincial governor of Hono no Kuni (present-day Higashi-Mikawa)}}. Katsuragi Sotsuhiko no Mikoto was the father of {{ill|lt=Iwa no Hime no Mikoto|Q1066593|WD=Q1066593}}, the empress of {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119}}, and is said to have wielded great power as a {{ill|lt=local ruling clan|Q84066|WD=Q84066}} of Katsuragi in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}}. After his death, Unakami Sukune no Mikoto was enshrined at Kashiwagi Beach in Hirai, {{ill|lt=Mikawa Province|Q768158|WD=Q768158}}, in recognition of his outstanding achievements in promoting industry and governing the people. It is said that during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181}}, in the 15th year of the {{ill|lt=Hakuhō period|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612}} ({{ill|lt=686|Q30619|WD=Q30619}}), on {{ill|lt=April 11|Q2501|WD=Q2501}}, the shrine was relocated to its present site by Hata no Ishikatsu (はたのいしかつ). In the 6th year of the {{ill|lt=Jōgan era (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635}} ({{ill|lt=864|Q25041|WD=Q25041}}), the shrine’s {{ill|lt=divine rank|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025}} was promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and its name appears in the Jinmyōchō of the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}. During the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707}}, when the Emperor made his eastern progress, the shrine received an imperial envoy. In the 11th year of the {{ill|lt=Meiji era|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} ({{ill|lt=1878|Q7776|WD=Q7776}}), it received a shrine name scroll in the imperial handwriting of {{ill|lt=Prince Arisugawa Taruhito|Q668338|WD=Q668338}}. In the 7th year of the {{ill|lt=Taishō era|Q329174|WD=Q329174}} ({{ill|lt=1918|Q2094|WD=Q2094}}), it was elevated from a village shrine to a prefectural shrine. === Festivals === * Wind Festival (Kazematsuri) – Held every year on the second Saturday and Sunday of April. {{ill|lt=Tezutsu fireworks|Q11497105|WD=Q11497105}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=launched fireworks|1=UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill|lt=festival floats|Q11470974|WD=Q11470974}} are dedicated. This festival is the origin of the name {{ill|lt=Kozakai Aoi Fūkan|Q108765371|WD=Q108765371}}. * Rice Paddy Festival – Held on the 7th day of the 1st month of the lunar calendar. du culte).jpg|250px]]
Worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=230}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434}}{{ill|lt=Toyokawa City|Q389495|WD=Q389495}} Kosakai-chō, Aza Miyawaki 2-1 |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|47|50.5|N|137|21|56.9|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Utari Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = Unakami Sukune no Mikoto |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (Minor)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |Founded = (Traditionally) {{ill|lt=Hakuhō period|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612}} year 15 ({{ill|lt=686|Q30619|WD=Q30619}}) |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage status = |Annual festival = Second {{ill|lt=Saturday|Q131|WD=Q131|lt=Sat}} and {{ill|lt=Sunday|Q132|WD=Q132}} of {{ill|lt=April|Q118|WD=Q118}} |Rituals = Wind Festival, Rice Paddy Festival |Map = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Utari Shrine|position=bottom|width=180|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Utari Shrine''' (うたりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Kosakai-chō, Aza Miyawaki, {{ill|lt=Toyokawa City|Q389495|WD=Q389495}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}. Under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking}}, it was designated as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines the following single deity: * Unakami Sukune no Mikoto (うなかみすくねのみこと) === History === The enshrined deity, Unakami Sukune no Mikoto, is said to be a descendant of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōgen|Q333572|WD=Q333572}}, the great-great-grandchild (fourth generation) of {{ill|lt=Katsuragi Sotsuhiko no Mikoto|Q11620922|WD=Q11620922}}. During the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235|WD=Q357235}}, he was appointed as {{ill|lt=Hono no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11597049|WD=Q11597049|lt=provincial governor of Hono no Kuni (present-day Higashi-Mikawa)}}. Katsuragi Sotsuhiko no Mikoto was the father of {{ill|lt=Iwa no Hime no Mikoto|Q1066593|WD=Q1066593}}, the empress of {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119}}, and is said to have wielded great power as a {{ill|lt=local ruling clan|Q84066|WD=Q84066}} of Katsuragi in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}}. After his death, Unakami Sukune no Mikoto was enshrined at Kashiwagi Beach in Hirai, {{ill|lt=Mikawa Province|Q768158|WD=Q768158}}, in recognition of his outstanding achievements in promoting industry and governing the people. It is said that during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181}}, in the 15th year of the {{ill|lt=Hakuhō period|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612}} ({{ill|lt=686|Q30619|WD=Q30619}}), on {{ill|lt=April 11|Q2501|WD=Q2501}}, the shrine was relocated to its present site by Hata no Ishikatsu (はたのいしかつ). In the 6th year of the {{ill|lt=Jōgan era (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635}} ({{ill|lt=864|Q25041|WD=Q25041}}), the shrine’s {{ill|lt=divine rank|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025}} was promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and its name appears in the Jinmyōchō of the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}. During the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707}}, when the Emperor made his eastern progress, the shrine received an imperial envoy. In the 11th year of the {{ill|lt=Meiji era|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} ({{ill|lt=1878|Q7776|WD=Q7776}}), it received a shrine name scroll in the imperial handwriting of {{ill|lt=Prince Arisugawa Taruhito|Q668338|WD=Q668338}}. In the 7th year of the {{ill|lt=Taishō era|Q329174|WD=Q329174}} ({{ill|lt=1918|Q2094|WD=Q2094}}), it was elevated from a village shrine to a prefectural shrine. === Festivals === * Wind Festival (Kazematsuri) – Held every year on the second Saturday and Sunday of April. {{ill|lt=Tezutsu fireworks|Q11497105|WD=Q11497105}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=launched fireworks|1=UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill|lt=festival floats|Q11470974|WD=Q11470974}} are dedicated. This festival is the origin of the name {{ill|lt=Kozakai Aoi Fūkan|Q108765371|WD=Q108765371}}. * Rice Paddy Festival – Held on the 7th day of the 1st month of the lunar calendar. File:Utari Shrine Chigo Dance.JPG|Chigo dance at Utari Shrine File:Utari Shrine Festival Float.JPG|Festival float at Utari Shrine File:Utari Shrine Tezutsu Fireworks 3.JPG|Tezutsu fireworks at Utari Shrine File:Utari Shrine Tezutsu Fireworks.JPG|Tezutsu fireworks at Utari Shrine File:Utari Shrine Tezutsu Fireworks Hane.JPG|Tezutsu fireworks sparks at Utari Shrine File:Utari Shrine Tezutsu Fireworks 2.JPG|Tezutsu fireworks at Utari Shrine File:Tezutsu Fireworks.JPG|Utari Shrine, Toyokawa City, Aichi Prefecture File:Utari Shrine Tezutsu Fireworks 4.JPG|Tezutsu fireworks at Utari Shrine === Cultural properties === ; Important Cultural Properties (National designation) * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Daihannya Sutra|1=UNKNOWN}} (printed edition, 9 volumes) 585 volumes, sutras copied in a single hand by Ken’ichi from Angen 2 to Jishō 3 – Designated on {{ill|lt=June 30|Q2657|WD=Q2657}}, {{ill|lt=1961|Q3696|WD=Q3696}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa era|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 36) ; Tangible Cultural Properties designated by Aichi Prefecture * {{ill|lt=Buddhist bell|Q8192099|WD=Q8192099}} – Designated on {{ill|lt=October 14|Q2922|WD=Q2922}}, {{ill|lt=1964|Q2652|WD=Q2652}} (Shōwa 39) * Utari Shrine Festival Masks (5 masks) – Designated on {{ill|lt=May 21|Q2580|WD=Q2580}}, {{ill|lt=1965|Q2650|WD=Q2650}} (Shōwa 40) ; Intangible Folk Cultural Property designated by Aichi Prefecture * Utari Shrine Rice Paddy Festival – {{ill|lt=1954|Q18581|WD=Q18581}} (Shōwa 29), {{ill|lt=March 12|Q2402|WD=Q2402}} === On-site information === ; Location * Kosakai-chō, Aza Miyawaki 2-1, {{ill|lt=Toyokawa City|Q389495|WD=Q389495}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434}} ; Access (public transportation – rail) * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Central Japan Railway Company|Q513679|WD=Q513679|lt=JR}} {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line (Nagoya area)|Q11235139|WD=Q11235139}} {{ill|lt=Kozakai Station|Q771874|WD=Q771874}}; about a 5-minute walk. * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850|lt=Meitetsu}} {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|WD=Q1137838}} {{ill|lt=Ina Station|Q6013231|WD=Q6013231}}; about a 20-minute walk. === Shrine grounds === File:Le Temple Shintô Utari-jinja - Le torii d'entrée.jpg|Stone torii gate at the entrance File:Le Temple Shintô Utari-jinja - Le temizuya.jpg|Purification font (temizuya) File:Le Temple Shintô Utari-jinja - Le shamu-sho.jpg|Shrine office File:Le Temple Shintô Utari-jinja - Le sanctuaire portable du lapin.jpg|Rabbit portable shrine inside the worship hall File:Le Temple Shintô Utari-jinja - La salle "Utari-kaikan".jpg|Utari Hall === Related literature === * “Utari Shrine Shirube” – Utari Shrine Office === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === Related topics === * {{ill|lt=Xu Fu|Q700302|WD=Q700302|lt=Legend of Xu Fu}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gosha Inari Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} === External links === {{commonscat|Utari-jinja}} * [https://www.toyokawa-map.net/spot/000291.html Utari Shrine] – Toyokawa City Tourism Association * [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=54zc-PluUGM Utari Shrine Wind Festival Tezutsu Fireworks [Toyokawa] [2013-04-13]] – YouTube {{Shinto sidebar}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Utari Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Utari Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Utari Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mikawa Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:愛知県の神社]] [[Category:豊川市の歴史]] [[Category:三河国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧県社]] [[Category:愛知県の重要文化財]] [[Category:豊川市の建築物]]

category|Category:Utari-jinja}} [[JA:菟足神社]] {{wikidata link|Q17216999}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Utari Shrine |Image = [[File:Le Temple Shintô Utari-jinja - Le haiden (La construction du culte).jpg|250px]]
Worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=230}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Toyokawa City|Q389495|WD=Q389495|1=Toyokawa, Aichi}} Kosakai-chō, Aza Miyawaki 2-1 |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|47|50.5|N|137|21|56.9|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Utari Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = Unakami Sukune no Mikoto |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (Minor)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |Founded = (Traditionally) {{ill|lt=Hakuhō period|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} year 15 ({{ill|lt=686|Q30619|WD=Q30619|1=686}}) |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage status = |Annual festival = Second {{ill|lt=Saturday|Q131|WD=Q131|lt=Sat|1=Saturday}} and {{ill|lt=Sunday|Q132|WD=Q132|1=Sunday}} of {{ill|lt=April|Q118|WD=Q118|1=April}} |Rituals = Wind Festival, Rice Paddy Festival |Map = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Utari Shrine|position=bottom|width=180|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Utari Shrine''' (うたりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Kosakai-chō, Aza Miyawaki, {{ill|lt=Toyokawa City|Q389495|WD=Q389495|1=Toyokawa, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was designated as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines the following single deity: * Unakami Sukune no Mikoto (うなかみすくねのみこと) === History === The enshrined deity, Unakami Sukune no Mikoto, is said to be a descendant of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōgen|Q333572|WD=Q333572|1=Emperor Kōgen}}, the great-great-grandchild (fourth generation) of {{ill|lt=Katsuragi Sotsuhiko no Mikoto|Q11620922|WD=Q11620922|1=Katsuragi Sotsuhiko}}. During the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235|WD=Q357235|1=Emperor Yūryaku}}, he was appointed as {{ill|lt=Hono no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11597049|WD=Q11597049|lt=provincial governor of Hono no Kuni (present-day Higashi-Mikawa)|1=provincial governor of Hono no Kuni (present-day Higashi-Mikawa)}}. Katsuragi Sotsuhiko no Mikoto was the father of {{ill|lt=Iwa no Hime no Mikoto|Q1066593|WD=Q1066593|1=Princess Iwa}}, the empress of {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}}, and is said to have wielded great power as a {{ill|lt=local ruling clan|Q84066|WD=Q84066|1=Gōzoku}} of Katsuragi in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}. After his death, Unakami Sukune no Mikoto was enshrined at Kashiwagi Beach in Hirai, {{ill|lt=Mikawa Province|Q768158|WD=Q768158|1=Mikawa Province}}, in recognition of his outstanding achievements in promoting industry and governing the people. It is said that during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}}, in the 15th year of the {{ill|lt=Hakuhō period|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} ({{ill|lt=686|Q30619|WD=Q30619|1=686}}), on {{ill|lt=April 11|Q2501|WD=Q2501|1=April 11}}, the shrine was relocated to its present site by Hata no Ishikatsu (はたのいしかつ). In the 6th year of the {{ill|lt=Jōgan era (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|1=Jōgan}} ({{ill|lt=864|Q25041|WD=Q25041|1=864}}), the shrine’s {{ill|lt=divine rank|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} was promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and its name appears in the Jinmyōchō of the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}. During the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, when the Emperor made his eastern progress, the shrine received an imperial envoy. In the 11th year of the {{ill|lt=Meiji era|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} ({{ill|lt=1878|Q7776|WD=Q7776|1=1878}}), it received a shrine name scroll in the imperial handwriting of {{ill|lt=Prince Arisugawa Taruhito|Q668338|WD=Q668338|1=Prince Arisugawa Taruhito}}. In the 7th year of the {{ill|lt=Taishō era|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} ({{ill|lt=1918|Q2094|WD=Q2094|1=1918}}), it was elevated from a village shrine to a prefectural shrine. === Festivals === * Wind Festival (Kazematsuri) – Held every year on the second Saturday and Sunday of April. {{ill|lt=Tezutsu fireworks|Q11497105|WD=Q11497105|1=Tezutsu-hanabi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=launched fireworks|1=UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill|lt=festival floats|Q11470974|WD=Q11470974|1=Matsuri float}} are dedicated. This festival is the origin of the name {{ill|lt=Kozakai Aoi Fūkan|Q108765371|WD=Q108765371|1=Kozakai Kifukan}}. * Rice Paddy Festival – Held on the 7th day of the 1st month of the lunar calendar. Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |Founded = (Traditionally) {{ill|lt=Hakuhō period|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} year 15 ({{ill|lt=686|Q30619|WD=Q30619|1=686}}) |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage status = |Annual festival = Second {{ill|lt=Saturday|Q131|WD=Q131|lt=Sat|1=Saturday}} and {{ill|lt=Sunday|Q132|WD=Q132|1=Sunday}} of {{ill|lt=April|Q118|WD=Q118|1=April}} |Rituals = Wind Festival, Rice Paddy Festival |Map = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Utari Shrine|position=bottom|width=180|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Utari Shrine''' (うたりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Kosakai-chō, Aza Miyawaki, {{ill|lt=Toyokawa City|Q389495|WD=Q389495|1=Toyokawa, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was designated as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines the following single deity: * Unakami Sukune no Mikoto (うなかみすくねのみこと) === History === The enshrined deity, Unakami Sukune no Mikoto, is said to be a descendant of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōgen|Q333572|WD=Q333572|1=Emperor Kōgen}}, the great-great-grandchild (fourth generation) of {{ill|lt=Katsuragi Sotsuhiko no Mikoto|Q11620922|WD=Q11620922|1=Katsuragi Sotsuhiko}}. During the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235|WD=Q357235|1=Emperor Yūryaku}}, he was appointed as {{ill|lt=Hono no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11597049|WD=Q11597049|lt=provincial governor of Hono no Kuni (present-day Higashi-Mikawa)|1=provincial governor of Hono no Kuni (present-day Higashi-Mikawa)}}. Katsuragi Sotsuhiko no Mikoto was the father of {{ill|lt=Iwa no Hime no Mikoto|Q1066593|WD=Q1066593|1=Princess Iwa}}, the empress of {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}}, and is said to have wielded great power as a {{ill|lt=local ruling clan|Q84066|WD=Q84066|1=Gōzoku}} of Katsuragi in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}. After his death, Unakami Sukune no Mikoto was enshrined at Kashiwagi Beach in Hirai, {{ill|lt=Mikawa Province|Q768158|WD=Q768158|1=Mikawa Province}}, in recognition of his outstanding achievements in promoting industry and governing the people. It is said that during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}}, in the 15th year of the {{ill|lt=Hakuhō period|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} ({{ill|lt=686|Q30619|WD=Q30619|1=686}}), on {{ill|lt=April 11|Q2501|WD=Q2501|1=April 11}}, the shrine was relocated to its present site by Hata no Ishikatsu (はたのいしかつ). In the 6th year of the {{ill|lt=Jōgan era (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|1=Jōgan}} ({{ill|lt=864|Q25041|WD=Q25041|1=864}}), the shrine’s {{ill|lt=divine rank|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} was promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and its name appears in the Jinmyōchō of the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}. During the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, when the Emperor made his eastern progress, the shrine received an imperial envoy. In the 11th year of the {{ill|lt=Meiji era|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} ({{ill|lt=1878|Q7776|WD=Q7776|1=1878}}), it received a shrine name scroll in the imperial handwriting of {{ill|lt=Prince Arisugawa Taruhito|Q668338|WD=Q668338|1=Prince Arisugawa Taruhito}}. In the 7th year of the {{ill|lt=Taishō era|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} ({{ill|lt=1918|Q2094|WD=Q2094|1=1918}}), it was elevated from a village shrine to a prefectural shrine. === Festivals === * Wind Festival (Kazematsuri) – Held every year on the second Saturday and Sunday of April. {{ill|lt=Tezutsu fireworks|Q11497105|WD=Q11497105|1=Tezutsu-hanabi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=launched fireworks|1=UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill|lt=festival floats|Q11470974|WD=Q11470974|1=Matsuri float}} are dedicated. This festival is the origin of the name {{ill|lt=Kozakai Aoi Fūkan|Q108765371|WD=Q108765371|1=Kozakai Kifukan}}. * Rice Paddy Festival – Held on the 7th day of the 1st month of the lunar calendar. File:Utari Shrine Chigo Dance.JPG|Chigo dance at Utari Shrine File:Utari Shrine Festival Float.JPG|Festival float at Utari Shrine File:Utari Shrine Tezutsu Fireworks 3.JPG|Tezutsu fireworks at Utari Shrine File:Utari Shrine Tezutsu Fireworks.JPG|Tezutsu fireworks at Utari Shrine File:Utari Shrine Tezutsu Fireworks Hane.JPG|Tezutsu fireworks sparks at Utari Shrine File:Utari Shrine Tezutsu Fireworks 2.JPG|Tezutsu fireworks at Utari Shrine File:Tezutsu Fireworks.JPG|Utari Shrine, Toyokawa City, Aichi Prefecture File:Utari Shrine Tezutsu Fireworks 4.JPG|Tezutsu fireworks at Utari Shrine === Cultural properties === ; Important Cultural Properties (National designation) * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Daihannya Sutra|1=UNKNOWN}} (printed edition, 9 volumes) 585 volumes, sutras copied in a single hand by Ken’ichi from Angen 2 to Jishō 3 – Designated on {{ill|lt=June 30|Q2657|WD=Q2657|1=June 30}}, {{ill|lt=1961|Q3696|WD=Q3696|1=1961}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa era|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 36) ; Tangible Cultural Properties designated by Aichi Prefecture * {{ill|lt=Buddhist bell|Q8192099|WD=Q8192099|1=Bonshō}} – Designated on {{ill|lt=October 14|Q2922|WD=Q2922|1=October 14}}, {{ill|lt=1964|Q2652|WD=Q2652|1=1964}} (Shōwa 39) * Utari Shrine Festival Masks (5 masks) – Designated on {{ill|lt=May 21|Q2580|WD=Q2580|1=May 21}}, {{ill|lt=1965|Q2650|WD=Q2650|1=1965}} (Shōwa 40) ; Intangible Folk Cultural Property designated by Aichi Prefecture * Utari Shrine Rice Paddy Festival – {{ill|lt=1954|Q18581|WD=Q18581|1=1954}} (Shōwa 29), {{ill|lt=March 12|Q2402|WD=Q2402|1=March 12}} === On-site information === ; Location * Kosakai-chō, Aza Miyawaki 2-1, {{ill|lt=Toyokawa City|Q389495|WD=Q389495|1=Toyokawa, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}} ; Access (public transportation – rail) * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Central Japan Railway Company|Q513679|WD=Q513679|lt=JR|1=Central Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line (Nagoya area)|Q11235139|WD=Q11235139|1=Tōkaidō Line (JR Central)}} {{ill|lt=Kozakai Station|Q771874|WD=Q771874|1=Kozakai Station}}; about a 5-minute walk. * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850|lt=Meitetsu|1=Meitetsu}} {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|WD=Q1137838|1=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line}} {{ill|lt=Ina Station|Q6013231|WD=Q6013231|1=Ina Station}}; about a 20-minute walk. === Shrine grounds === File:Le Temple Shintô Utari-jinja - Le torii d'entrée.jpg|Stone torii gate at the entrance File:Le Temple Shintô Utari-jinja - Le temizuya.jpg|Purification font (temizuya) File:Le Temple Shintô Utari-jinja - Le shamu-sho.jpg|Shrine office File:Le Temple Shintô Utari-jinja - Le sanctuaire portable du lapin.jpg|Rabbit portable shrine inside the worship hall File:Le Temple Shintô Utari-jinja - La salle "Utari-kaikan".jpg|Utari Hall === Related literature === * “Utari Shrine Shirube” – Utari Shrine Office === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === Related topics === * {{ill|lt=Xu Fu|Q700302|WD=Q700302|lt=Legend of Xu Fu|1=Xu Fu}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gosha Inari Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} === External links === {{commonscat|Utari-jinja}} * [https://www.toyokawa-map.net/spot/000291.html Utari Shrine] – Toyokawa City Tourism Association * [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=54zc-PluUGM Utari Shrine Wind Festival Tezutsu Fireworks [Toyokawa] [2013-04-13]] – YouTube {{Shinto sidebar}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Utari Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Utari Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Utari Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mikawa Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:愛知県の神社]] [[Category:豊川市の歴史]] [[Category:三河国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧県社]] [[Category:愛知県の重要文化財]] [[Category:豊川市の建築物]]

du culte).jpg|250px]]
Worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=230}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Toyokawa City|Q389495|WD=Q389495|1=Toyokawa, Aichi}} Kosakai-chō, Aza Miyawaki 2-1 |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|47|50.5|N|137|21|56.9|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Utari Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = Unakami Sukune no Mikoto |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (Minor)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |Founded = (Traditionally) {{ill|lt=Hakuhō period|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} year 15 ({{ill|lt=686|Q30619|WD=Q30619|1=686}}) |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage status = |Annual festival = Second {{ill|lt=Saturday|Q131|WD=Q131|lt=Sat|1=Saturday}} and {{ill|lt=Sunday|Q132|WD=Q132|1=Sunday}} of {{ill|lt=April|Q118|WD=Q118|1=April}} |Rituals = Wind Festival, Rice Paddy Festival |Map = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Utari Shrine|position=bottom|width=180|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Utari Shrine''' (うたりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Kosakai-chō, Aza Miyawaki, {{ill|lt=Toyokawa City|Q389495|WD=Q389495|1=Toyokawa, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was designated as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines the following single deity: * Unakami Sukune no Mikoto (うなかみすくねのみこと) === History === The enshrined deity, Unakami Sukune no Mikoto, is said to be a descendant of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōgen|Q333572|WD=Q333572|1=Emperor Kōgen}}, the great-great-grandchild (fourth generation) of {{ill|lt=Katsuragi Sotsuhiko no Mikoto|Q11620922|WD=Q11620922|1=Katsuragi Sotsuhiko}}. During the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235|WD=Q357235|1=Emperor Yūryaku}}, he was appointed as {{ill|lt=Hono no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11597049|WD=Q11597049|lt=provincial governor of Hono no Kuni (present-day Higashi-Mikawa)|1=provincial governor of Hono no Kuni (present-day Higashi-Mikawa)}}. Katsuragi Sotsuhiko no Mikoto was the father of {{ill|lt=Iwa no Hime no Mikoto|Q1066593|WD=Q1066593|1=Princess Iwa}}, the empress of {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}}, and is said to have wielded great power as a {{ill|lt=local ruling clan|Q84066|WD=Q84066|1=Gōzoku}} of Katsuragi in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}. After his death, Unakami Sukune no Mikoto was enshrined at Kashiwagi Beach in Hirai, {{ill|lt=Mikawa Province|Q768158|WD=Q768158|1=Mikawa Province}}, in recognition of his outstanding achievements in promoting industry and governing the people. It is said that during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}}, in the 15th year of the {{ill|lt=Hakuhō period|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} ({{ill|lt=686|Q30619|WD=Q30619|1=686}}), on {{ill|lt=April 11|Q2501|WD=Q2501|1=April 11}}, the shrine was relocated to its present site by Hata no Ishikatsu (はたのいしかつ). In the 6th year of the {{ill|lt=Jōgan era (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|1=Jōgan}} ({{ill|lt=864|Q25041|WD=Q25041|1=864}}), the shrine’s {{ill|lt=divine rank|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} was promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and its name appears in the Jinmyōchō of the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}. During the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, when the Emperor made his eastern progress, the shrine received an imperial envoy. In the 11th year of the {{ill|lt=Meiji era|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} ({{ill|lt=1878|Q7776|WD=Q7776|1=1878}}), it received a shrine name scroll in the imperial handwriting of {{ill|lt=Prince Arisugawa Taruhito|Q668338|WD=Q668338|1=Prince Arisugawa Taruhito}}. In the 7th year of the {{ill|lt=Taishō era|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} ({{ill|lt=1918|Q2094|WD=Q2094|1=1918}}), it was elevated from a village shrine to a prefectural shrine. === Festivals === * Wind Festival (Kazematsuri) – Held every year on the second Saturday and Sunday of April. {{ill|lt=Tezutsu fireworks|Q11497105|WD=Q11497105|1=Tezutsu-hanabi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=launched fireworks|1=UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill|lt=festival floats|Q11470974|WD=Q11470974|1=Matsuri float}} are dedicated. This festival is the origin of the name {{ill|lt=Kozakai Aoi Fūkan|Q108765371|WD=Q108765371|1=Kozakai Kifukan}}. * Rice Paddy Festival – Held on the 7th day of the 1st month of the lunar calendar. File:Utari Shrine Chigo Dance.JPG|Chigo dance at Utari Shrine File:Utari Shrine Festival Float.JPG|Festival float at Utari Shrine File:Utari Shrine Tezutsu Fireworks 3.JPG|Tezutsu fireworks at Utari Shrine File:Utari Shrine Tezutsu Fireworks.JPG|Tezutsu fireworks at Utari Shrine File:Utari Shrine Tezutsu Fireworks Hane.JPG|Tezutsu fireworks sparks at Utari Shrine File:Utari Shrine Tezutsu Fireworks 2.JPG|Tezutsu fireworks at Utari Shrine File:Tezutsu Fireworks.JPG|Utari Shrine, Toyokawa City, Aichi Prefecture File:Utari Shrine Tezutsu Fireworks 4.JPG|Tezutsu fireworks at Utari Shrine === Cultural properties === ; Important Cultural Properties (National designation) * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Daihannya Sutra|1=UNKNOWN}} (printed edition, 9 volumes) 585 volumes, sutras copied in a single hand by Ken’ichi from Angen 2 to Jishō 3 – Designated on {{ill|lt=June 30|Q2657|WD=Q2657|1=June 30}}, {{ill|lt=1961|Q3696|WD=Q3696|1=1961}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa era|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 36) ; Tangible Cultural Properties designated by Aichi Prefecture * {{ill|lt=Buddhist bell|Q8192099|WD=Q8192099|1=Bonshō}} – Designated on {{ill|lt=October 14|Q2922|WD=Q2922|1=October 14}}, {{ill|lt=1964|Q2652|WD=Q2652|1=1964}} (Shōwa 39) * Utari Shrine Festival Masks (5 masks) – Designated on {{ill|lt=May 21|Q2580|WD=Q2580|1=May 21}}, {{ill|lt=1965|Q2650|WD=Q2650|1=1965}} (Shōwa 40) ; Intangible Folk Cultural Property designated by Aichi Prefecture * Utari Shrine Rice Paddy Festival – {{ill|lt=1954|Q18581|WD=Q18581|1=1954}} (Shōwa 29), {{ill|lt=March 12|Q2402|WD=Q2402|1=March 12}} === On-site information === ; Location * Kosakai-chō, Aza Miyawaki 2-1, {{ill|lt=Toyokawa City|Q389495|WD=Q389495|1=Toyokawa, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}} ; Access (public transportation – rail) * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Central Japan Railway Company|Q513679|WD=Q513679|lt=JR|1=Central Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line (Nagoya area)|Q11235139|WD=Q11235139|1=Tōkaidō Line (JR Central)}} {{ill|lt=Kozakai Station|Q771874|WD=Q771874|1=Kozakai Station}}; about a 5-minute walk. * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850|lt=Meitetsu|1=Meitetsu}} {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|WD=Q1137838|1=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line}} {{ill|lt=Ina Station|Q6013231|WD=Q6013231|1=Ina Station}}; about a 20-minute walk. === Shrine grounds === File:Le Temple Shintô Utari-jinja - Le torii d'entrée.jpg|Stone torii gate at the entrance File:Le Temple Shintô Utari-jinja - Le temizuya.jpg|Purification font (temizuya) File:Le Temple Shintô Utari-jinja - Le shamu-sho.jpg|Shrine office File:Le Temple Shintô Utari-jinja - Le sanctuaire portable du lapin.jpg|Rabbit portable shrine inside the worship hall File:Le Temple Shintô Utari-jinja - La salle "Utari-kaikan".jpg|Utari Hall === Related literature === * “Utari Shrine Shirube” – Utari Shrine Office === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === Related topics === * {{ill|lt=Xu Fu|Q700302|WD=Q700302|lt=Legend of Xu Fu|1=Xu Fu}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gosha Inari Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} === External links === {{commonscat|Utari-jinja}} * [https://www.toyokawa-map.net/spot/000291.html Utari Shrine] – Toyokawa City Tourism Association * [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=54zc-PluUGM Utari Shrine Wind Festival Tezutsu Fireworks [Toyokawa] [2013-04-13]] – YouTube {{Shinto sidebar}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Utari Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Utari Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mikawa Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:愛知県の神社]] [[Category:豊川市の歴史]] [[Category:三河国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧県社]] [[Category:愛知県の重要文化財]] [[Category:豊川市の建築物]]

category|Category:Utari-jinja}} [[JA:菟足神社]] {{wikidata link|Q17216999}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Utari Shrine |Image = [[File:Le Temple Shintô Utari-jinja - Le haiden (La construction du culte).jpg|250px]]
Worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=230}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Toyokawa City|Q389495|WD=Q389495|1=Toyokawa, Aichi}} Kosakai-chō, Aza Miyawaki 2-1 |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|47|50.5|N|137|21|56.9|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Utari Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = Unakami Sukune no Mikoto |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (Minor)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |Founded = (Traditionally) {{ill|lt=Hakuhō period|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} year 15 ({{ill|lt=686|Q30619|WD=Q30619|1=686}}) |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage status = |Annual festival = Second {{ill|lt=Saturday|Q131|WD=Q131|lt=Sat|1=Saturday}} and {{ill|lt=Sunday|Q132|WD=Q132|1=Sunday}} of {{ill|lt=April|Q118|WD=Q118|1=April}} |Rituals = Wind Festival, Rice Paddy Festival |Map = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Utari Shrine|position=bottom|width=180|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Utari Shrine''' (うたりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Kosakai-chō, Aza Miyawaki, {{ill|lt=Toyokawa City|Q389495|WD=Q389495|1=Toyokawa, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was designated as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines the following single deity: * Unakami Sukune no Mikoto (うなかみすくねのみこと) === History === The enshrined deity, Unakami Sukune no Mikoto, is said to be a descendant of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōgen|Q333572|WD=Q333572|1=Emperor Kōgen}}, the great-great-grandchild (fourth generation) of {{ill|lt=Katsuragi Sotsuhiko no Mikoto|Q11620922|WD=Q11620922|1=Katsuragi Sotsuhiko}}. During the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235|WD=Q357235|1=Emperor Yūryaku}}, he was appointed as {{ill|lt=Hono no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11597049|WD=Q11597049|lt=provincial governor of Hono no Kuni (present-day Higashi-Mikawa)|1=provincial governor of Hono no Kuni (present-day Higashi-Mikawa)}}. Katsuragi Sotsuhiko no Mikoto was the father of {{ill|lt=Iwa no Hime no Mikoto|Q1066593|WD=Q1066593|1=Princess Iwa}}, the empress of {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}}, and is said to have wielded great power as a {{ill|lt=local ruling clan|Q84066|WD=Q84066|1=Gōzoku}} of Katsuragi in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}. After his death, Unakami Sukune no Mikoto was enshrined at Kashiwagi Beach in Hirai, {{ill|lt=Mikawa Province|Q768158|WD=Q768158|1=Mikawa Province}}, in recognition of his outstanding achievements in promoting industry and governing the people. It is said that during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}}, in the 15th year of the {{ill|lt=Hakuhō period|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} ({{ill|lt=686|Q30619|WD=Q30619|1=686}}), on {{ill|lt=April 11|Q2501|WD=Q2501|1=April 11}}, the shrine was relocated to its present site by Hata no Ishikatsu (はたのいしかつ). In the 6th year of the {{ill|lt=Jōgan era (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|1=Jōgan}} ({{ill|lt=864|Q25041|WD=Q25041|1=864}}), the shrine’s {{ill|lt=divine rank|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} was promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and its name appears in the Jinmyōchō of the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}. During the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, when the Emperor made his eastern progress, the shrine received an imperial envoy. In the 11th year of the {{ill|lt=Meiji era|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} ({{ill|lt=1878|Q7776|WD=Q7776|1=1878}}), it received a shrine name scroll in the imperial handwriting of {{ill|lt=Prince Arisugawa Taruhito|Q668338|WD=Q668338|1=Prince Arisugawa Taruhito}}. In the 7th year of the {{ill|lt=Taishō era|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} ({{ill|lt=1918|Q2094|WD=Q2094|1=1918}}), it was elevated from a village shrine to a prefectural shrine. === Festivals === * Wind Festival (Kazematsuri) – Held every year on the second Saturday and Sunday of April. {{ill|lt=Tezutsu fireworks|Q11497105|WD=Q11497105|1=Tezutsu-hanabi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=launched fireworks|1=UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill|lt=festival floats|Q11470974|WD=Q11470974|1=Matsuri float}} are dedicated. This festival is the origin of the name {{ill|lt=Kozakai Aoi Fūkan|Q108765371|WD=Q108765371|1=Kozakai Kifukan}}. * Rice Paddy Festival – Held on the 7th day of the 1st month of the lunar calendar. File:Utari Shrine Chigo Dance.JPG|Chigo dance at Utari Shrine File:Utari Shrine Festival Float.JPG|Festival float at Utari Shrine File:Utari Shrine Tezutsu Fireworks 3.JPG|Tezutsu fireworks at Utari Shrine File:Utari Shrine Tezutsu Fireworks.JPG|Tezutsu fireworks at Utari Shrine File:Utari Shrine Tezutsu Fireworks Hane.JPG|Tezutsu fireworks sparks at Utari Shrine File:Utari Shrine Tezutsu Fireworks 2.JPG|Tezutsu fireworks at Utari Shrine File:Tezutsu Fireworks.JPG|Utari Shrine, Toyokawa City, Aichi Prefecture File:Utari Shrine Tezutsu Fireworks 4.JPG|Tezutsu fireworks at Utari Shrine === Cultural properties === ; Important Cultural Properties (National designation) * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Daihannya Sutra|1=UNKNOWN}} (printed edition, 9 volumes) 585 volumes, sutras copied in a single hand by Ken’ichi from Angen 2 to Jishō 3 – Designated on {{ill|lt=June 30|Q2657|WD=Q2657|1=June 30}}, {{ill|lt=1961|Q3696|WD=Q3696|1=1961}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa era|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 36) ; Tangible Cultural Properties designated by Aichi Prefecture * {{ill|lt=Buddhist bell|Q8192099|WD=Q8192099|1=Bonshō}} – Designated on {{ill|lt=October 14|Q2922|WD=Q2922|1=October 14}}, {{ill|lt=1964|Q2652|WD=Q2652|1=1964}} (Shōwa 39) * Utari Shrine Festival Masks (5 masks) – Designated on {{ill|lt=May 21|Q2580|WD=Q2580|1=May 21}}, {{ill|lt=1965|Q2650|WD=Q2650|1=1965}} (Shōwa 40) ; Intangible Folk Cultural Property designated by Aichi Prefecture * Utari Shrine Rice Paddy Festival – {{ill|lt=1954|Q18581|WD=Q18581|1=1954}} (Shōwa 29), {{ill|lt=March 12|Q2402|WD=Q2402|1=March 12}} === On-site information === ; Location * Kosakai-chō, Aza Miyawaki 2-1, {{ill|lt=Toyokawa City|Q389495|WD=Q389495|1=Toyokawa, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}} ; Access (public transportation – rail) * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Central Japan Railway Company|Q513679|WD=Q513679|lt=JR|1=Central Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line (Nagoya area)|Q11235139|WD=Q11235139|1=Tōkaidō Line (JR Central)}} {{ill|lt=Kozakai Station|Q771874|WD=Q771874|1=Kozakai Station}}; about a 5-minute walk. * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850|lt=Meitetsu|1=Meitetsu}} {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|WD=Q1137838|1=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line}} {{ill|lt=Ina Station|Q6013231|WD=Q6013231|1=Ina Station}}; about a 20-minute walk. === Shrine grounds === File:Le Temple Shintô Utari-jinja - Le torii d'entrée.jpg|Stone torii gate at the entrance File:Le Temple Shintô Utari-jinja - Le temizuya.jpg|Purification font (temizuya) File:Le Temple Shintô Utari-jinja - Le shamu-sho.jpg|Shrine office File:Le Temple Shintô Utari-jinja - Le sanctuaire portable du lapin.jpg|Rabbit portable shrine inside the worship hall File:Le Temple Shintô Utari-jinja - La salle "Utari-kaikan".jpg|Utari Hall === Related literature === * “Utari Shrine Shirube” – Utari Shrine Office === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === Related topics === * {{ill|lt=Xu Fu|Q700302|WD=Q700302|lt=Legend of Xu Fu|1=Xu Fu}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gosha Inari Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} === External links === {{commonscat|Utari-jinja}} * [https://www.toyokawa-map.net/spot/000291.html Utari Shrine] – Toyokawa City Tourism Association * [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=54zc-PluUGM Utari Shrine Wind Festival Tezutsu Fireworks [Toyokawa] [2013-04-13]] – YouTube {{Shinto sidebar}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Utari Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Utari Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mikawa Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:愛知県の神社]] [[Category:豊川市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mikawa Province]] [[Category:愛知県の旧県社]] [[Category:愛知県の重要文化財]] [[Category:豊川市の建築物]]

category|Category:Utari-jinja}} [[JA:菟足神社]] {{wikidata link|Q17216999}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Utari Shrine |Image = [[File:Le Temple Shintô Utari-jinja - Le haiden (La construction du culte).jpg|250px]]
Worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=230}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Toyokawa City|Q389495|WD=Q389495|1=Toyokawa, Aichi}} Kosakai-chō, Aza Miyawaki 2-1 |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|47|50.5|N|137|21|56.9|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Utari Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = Unakami Sukune no Mikoto |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (Minor)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |Founded = (Traditionally) {{ill|lt=Hakuhō period|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} year 15 ({{ill|lt=686|Q30619|WD=Q30619|1=686}}) |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage status = |Annual festival = Second {{ill|lt=Saturday|Q131|WD=Q131|lt=Sat|1=Saturday}} and {{ill|lt=Sunday|Q132|WD=Q132|1=Sunday}} of {{ill|lt=April|Q118|WD=Q118|1=April}} |Rituals = Wind Festival, Rice Paddy Festival |Map = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Utari Shrine|position=bottom|width=180|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Utari Shrine''' (うたりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Kosakai-chō, Aza Miyawaki, {{ill|lt=Toyokawa City|Q389495|WD=Q389495|1=Toyokawa, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was designated as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines the following single deity: * Unakami Sukune no Mikoto (うなかみすくねのみこと) === History === The enshrined deity, Unakami Sukune no Mikoto, is said to be a descendant of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōgen|Q333572|WD=Q333572|1=Emperor Kōgen}}, the great-great-grandchild (fourth generation) of {{ill|lt=Katsuragi Sotsuhiko no Mikoto|Q11620922|WD=Q11620922|1=Katsuragi Sotsuhiko}}. During the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235|WD=Q357235|1=Emperor Yūryaku}}, he was appointed as {{ill|lt=Hono no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11597049|WD=Q11597049|lt=provincial governor of Hono no Kuni (present-day Higashi-Mikawa)|1=provincial governor of Hono no Kuni (present-day Higashi-Mikawa)}}. Katsuragi Sotsuhiko no Mikoto was the father of {{ill|lt=Iwa no Hime no Mikoto|Q1066593|WD=Q1066593|1=Princess Iwa}}, the empress of {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}}, and is said to have wielded great power as a {{ill|lt=local ruling clan|Q84066|WD=Q84066|1=Gōzoku}} of Katsuragi in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}. After his death, Unakami Sukune no Mikoto was enshrined at Kashiwagi Beach in Hirai, {{ill|lt=Mikawa Province|Q768158|WD=Q768158|1=Mikawa Province}}, in recognition of his outstanding achievements in promoting industry and governing the people. It is said that during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}}, in the 15th year of the {{ill|lt=Hakuhō period|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} ({{ill|lt=686|Q30619|WD=Q30619|1=686}}), on {{ill|lt=April 11|Q2501|WD=Q2501|1=April 11}}, the shrine was relocated to its present site by Hata no Ishikatsu (はたのいしかつ). In the 6th year of the {{ill|lt=Jōgan era (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|1=Jōgan}} ({{ill|lt=864|Q25041|WD=Q25041|1=864}}), the shrine’s {{ill|lt=divine rank|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} was promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and its name appears in the Jinmyōchō of the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}. During the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, when the Emperor made his eastern progress, the shrine received an imperial envoy. In the 11th year of the {{ill|lt=Meiji era|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} ({{ill|lt=1878|Q7776|WD=Q7776|1=1878}}), it received a shrine name scroll in the imperial handwriting of {{ill|lt=Prince Arisugawa Taruhito|Q668338|WD=Q668338|1=Prince Arisugawa Taruhito}}. In the 7th year of the {{ill|lt=Taishō era|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} ({{ill|lt=1918|Q2094|WD=Q2094|1=1918}}), it was elevated from a village shrine to a prefectural shrine. === Festivals === * Wind Festival (Kazematsuri) – Held every year on the second Saturday and Sunday of April. {{ill|lt=Tezutsu fireworks|Q11497105|WD=Q11497105|1=Tezutsu-hanabi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=launched fireworks|1=UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill|lt=festival floats|Q11470974|WD=Q11470974|1=Matsuri float}} are dedicated. This festival is the origin of the name {{ill|lt=Kozakai Aoi Fūkan|Q108765371|WD=Q108765371|1=Kozakai Kifukan}}. * Rice Paddy Festival – Held on the 7th day of the 1st month of the lunar calendar. File:Utari Shrine Chigo Dance.JPG|Chigo dance at Utari Shrine File:Utari Shrine Festival Float.JPG|Festival float at Utari Shrine File:Utari Shrine Tezutsu Fireworks 3.JPG|Tezutsu fireworks at Utari Shrine File:Utari Shrine Tezutsu Fireworks.JPG|Tezutsu fireworks at Utari Shrine File:Utari Shrine Tezutsu Fireworks Hane.JPG|Tezutsu fireworks sparks at Utari Shrine File:Utari Shrine Tezutsu Fireworks 2.JPG|Tezutsu fireworks at Utari Shrine File:Tezutsu Fireworks.JPG|Utari Shrine, Toyokawa City, Aichi Prefecture File:Utari Shrine Tezutsu Fireworks 4.JPG|Tezutsu fireworks at Utari Shrine === Cultural properties === ; Important Cultural Properties (National designation) * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Daihannya Sutra|1=UNKNOWN}} (printed edition, 9 volumes) 585 volumes, sutras copied in a single hand by Ken’ichi from Angen 2 to Jishō 3 – Designated on {{ill|lt=June 30|Q2657|WD=Q2657|1=June 30}}, {{ill|lt=1961|Q3696|WD=Q3696|1=1961}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa era|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 36) ; Tangible Cultural Properties designated by Aichi Prefecture * {{ill|lt=Buddhist bell|Q8192099|WD=Q8192099|1=Bonshō}} – Designated on {{ill|lt=October 14|Q2922|WD=Q2922|1=October 14}}, {{ill|lt=1964|Q2652|WD=Q2652|1=1964}} (Shōwa 39) * Utari Shrine Festival Masks (5 masks) – Designated on {{ill|lt=May 21|Q2580|WD=Q2580|1=May 21}}, {{ill|lt=1965|Q2650|WD=Q2650|1=1965}} (Shōwa 40) ; Intangible Folk Cultural Property designated by Aichi Prefecture * Utari Shrine Rice Paddy Festival – {{ill|lt=1954|Q18581|WD=Q18581|1=1954}} (Shōwa 29), {{ill|lt=March 12|Q2402|WD=Q2402|1=March 12}} === On-site information === ; Location * Kosakai-chō, Aza Miyawaki 2-1, {{ill|lt=Toyokawa City|Q389495|WD=Q389495|1=Toyokawa, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}} ; Access (public transportation – rail) * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Central Japan Railway Company|Q513679|WD=Q513679|lt=JR|1=Central Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line (Nagoya area)|Q11235139|WD=Q11235139|1=Tōkaidō Line (JR Central)}} {{ill|lt=Kozakai Station|Q771874|WD=Q771874|1=Kozakai Station}}; about a 5-minute walk. * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850|lt=Meitetsu|1=Meitetsu}} {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|WD=Q1137838|1=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line}} {{ill|lt=Ina Station|Q6013231|WD=Q6013231|1=Ina Station}}; about a 20-minute walk. === Shrine grounds === File:Le Temple Shintô Utari-jinja - Le torii d'entrée.jpg|Stone torii gate at the entrance File:Le Temple Shintô Utari-jinja - Le temizuya.jpg|Purification font (temizuya) File:Le Temple Shintô Utari-jinja - Le shamu-sho.jpg|Shrine office File:Le Temple Shintô Utari-jinja - Le sanctuaire portable du lapin.jpg|Rabbit portable shrine inside the worship hall File:Le Temple Shintô Utari-jinja - La salle "Utari-kaikan".jpg|Utari Hall === Related literature === * “Utari Shrine Shirube” – Utari Shrine Office === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === Related topics === * {{ill|lt=Xu Fu|Q700302|WD=Q700302|lt=Legend of Xu Fu|1=Xu Fu}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gosha Inari Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} === External links === {{commonscat|Utari-jinja}} * [https://www.toyokawa-map.net/spot/000291.html Utari Shrine] – Toyokawa City Tourism Association * [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=54zc-PluUGM Utari Shrine Wind Festival Tezutsu Fireworks [Toyokawa] [2013-04-13]] – YouTube {{Shinto sidebar}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Utari Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Utari Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mikawa Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:愛知県の神社]] [[Category:豊川市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mikawa Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:愛知県の重要文化財]] [[Category:豊川市の建築物]]

category|Category:Utari-jinja}} [[JA:菟足神社]] {{wikidata link|Q17216999}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Utari Shrine |Image = [[File:Le Temple Shintô Utari-jinja - Le haiden (La construction du culte).jpg|250px]]
Worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=230}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Toyokawa City|Q389495|WD=Q389495|1=Toyokawa, Aichi}} Kosakai-chō, Aza Miyawaki 2-1 |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|47|50.5|N|137|21|56.9|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Utari Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = Unakami Sukune no Mikoto |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (Minor)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |Founded = (Traditionally) {{ill|lt=Hakuhō period|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} year 15 ({{ill|lt=686|Q30619|WD=Q30619|1=686}}) |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage status = |Annual festival = Second {{ill|lt=Saturday|Q131|WD=Q131|lt=Sat|1=Saturday}} and {{ill|lt=Sunday|Q132|WD=Q132|1=Sunday}} of {{ill|lt=April|Q118|WD=Q118|1=April}} |Rituals = Wind Festival, Rice Paddy Festival |Map = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Utari Shrine|position=bottom|width=180|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Utari Shrine''' (うたりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Kosakai-chō, Aza Miyawaki, {{ill|lt=Toyokawa City|Q389495|WD=Q389495|1=Toyokawa, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was designated as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines the following single deity: * Unakami Sukune no Mikoto (うなかみすくねのみこと) === History === The enshrined deity, Unakami Sukune no Mikoto, is said to be a descendant of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōgen|Q333572|WD=Q333572|1=Emperor Kōgen}}, the great-great-grandchild (fourth generation) of {{ill|lt=Katsuragi Sotsuhiko no Mikoto|Q11620922|WD=Q11620922|1=Katsuragi Sotsuhiko}}. During the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235|WD=Q357235|1=Emperor Yūryaku}}, he was appointed as {{ill|lt=Hono no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11597049|WD=Q11597049|lt=provincial governor of Hono no Kuni (present-day Higashi-Mikawa)|1=provincial governor of Hono no Kuni (present-day Higashi-Mikawa)}}. Katsuragi Sotsuhiko no Mikoto was the father of {{ill|lt=Iwa no Hime no Mikoto|Q1066593|WD=Q1066593|1=Princess Iwa}}, the empress of {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}}, and is said to have wielded great power as a {{ill|lt=local ruling clan|Q84066|WD=Q84066|1=Gōzoku}} of Katsuragi in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}. After his death, Unakami Sukune no Mikoto was enshrined at Kashiwagi Beach in Hirai, {{ill|lt=Mikawa Province|Q768158|WD=Q768158|1=Mikawa Province}}, in recognition of his outstanding achievements in promoting industry and governing the people. It is said that during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}}, in the 15th year of the {{ill|lt=Hakuhō period|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} ({{ill|lt=686|Q30619|WD=Q30619|1=686}}), on {{ill|lt=April 11|Q2501|WD=Q2501|1=April 11}}, the shrine was relocated to its present site by Hata no Ishikatsu (はたのいしかつ). In the 6th year of the {{ill|lt=Jōgan era (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|1=Jōgan}} ({{ill|lt=864|Q25041|WD=Q25041|1=864}}), the shrine’s {{ill|lt=divine rank|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} was promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and its name appears in the Jinmyōchō of the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}. During the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, when the Emperor made his eastern progress, the shrine received an imperial envoy. In the 11th year of the {{ill|lt=Meiji era|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} ({{ill|lt=1878|Q7776|WD=Q7776|1=1878}}), it received a shrine name scroll in the imperial handwriting of {{ill|lt=Prince Arisugawa Taruhito|Q668338|WD=Q668338|1=Prince Arisugawa Taruhito}}. In the 7th year of the {{ill|lt=Taishō era|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} ({{ill|lt=1918|Q2094|WD=Q2094|1=1918}}), it was elevated from a village shrine to a prefectural shrine. === Festivals === * Wind Festival (Kazematsuri) – Held every year on the second Saturday and Sunday of April. {{ill|lt=Tezutsu fireworks|Q11497105|WD=Q11497105|1=Tezutsu-hanabi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=launched fireworks|1=UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill|lt=festival floats|Q11470974|WD=Q11470974|1=Matsuri float}} are dedicated. This festival is the origin of the name {{ill|lt=Kozakai Aoi Fūkan|Q108765371|WD=Q108765371|1=Kozakai Kifukan}}. * Rice Paddy Festival – Held on the 7th day of the 1st month of the lunar calendar. File:Utari Shrine Chigo Dance.JPG|Chigo dance at Utari Shrine File:Utari Shrine Festival Float.JPG|Festival float at Utari Shrine File:Utari Shrine Tezutsu Fireworks 3.JPG|Tezutsu fireworks at Utari Shrine File:Utari Shrine Tezutsu Fireworks.JPG|Tezutsu fireworks at Utari Shrine File:Utari Shrine Tezutsu Fireworks Hane.JPG|Tezutsu fireworks sparks at Utari Shrine File:Utari Shrine Tezutsu Fireworks 2.JPG|Tezutsu fireworks at Utari Shrine File:Tezutsu Fireworks.JPG|Utari Shrine, Toyokawa City, Aichi Prefecture File:Utari Shrine Tezutsu Fireworks 4.JPG|Tezutsu fireworks at Utari Shrine === Cultural properties === ; Important Cultural Properties (National designation) * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Daihannya Sutra|1=UNKNOWN}} (printed edition, 9 volumes) 585 volumes, sutras copied in a single hand by Ken’ichi from Angen 2 to Jishō 3 – Designated on {{ill|lt=June 30|Q2657|WD=Q2657|1=June 30}}, {{ill|lt=1961|Q3696|WD=Q3696|1=1961}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa era|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 36) ; Tangible Cultural Properties designated by Aichi Prefecture * {{ill|lt=Buddhist bell|Q8192099|WD=Q8192099|1=Bonshō}} – Designated on {{ill|lt=October 14|Q2922|WD=Q2922|1=October 14}}, {{ill|lt=1964|Q2652|WD=Q2652|1=1964}} (Shōwa 39) * Utari Shrine Festival Masks (5 masks) – Designated on {{ill|lt=May 21|Q2580|WD=Q2580|1=May 21}}, {{ill|lt=1965|Q2650|WD=Q2650|1=1965}} (Shōwa 40) ; Intangible Folk Cultural Property designated by Aichi Prefecture * Utari Shrine Rice Paddy Festival – {{ill|lt=1954|Q18581|WD=Q18581|1=1954}} (Shōwa 29), {{ill|lt=March 12|Q2402|WD=Q2402|1=March 12}} === On-site information === ; Location * Kosakai-chō, Aza Miyawaki 2-1, {{ill|lt=Toyokawa City|Q389495|WD=Q389495|1=Toyokawa, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}} ; Access (public transportation – rail) * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Central Japan Railway Company|Q513679|WD=Q513679|lt=JR|1=Central Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line (Nagoya area)|Q11235139|WD=Q11235139|1=Tōkaidō Line (JR Central)}} {{ill|lt=Kozakai Station|Q771874|WD=Q771874|1=Kozakai Station}}; about a 5-minute walk. * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850|lt=Meitetsu|1=Meitetsu}} {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|WD=Q1137838|1=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line}} {{ill|lt=Ina Station|Q6013231|WD=Q6013231|1=Ina Station}}; about a 20-minute walk. === Shrine grounds === File:Le Temple Shintô Utari-jinja - Le torii d'entrée.jpg|Stone torii gate at the entrance File:Le Temple Shintô Utari-jinja - Le temizuya.jpg|Purification font (temizuya) File:Le Temple Shintô Utari-jinja - Le shamu-sho.jpg|Shrine office File:Le Temple Shintô Utari-jinja - Le sanctuaire portable du lapin.jpg|Rabbit portable shrine inside the worship hall File:Le Temple Shintô Utari-jinja - La salle "Utari-kaikan".jpg|Utari Hall === Related literature === * “Utari Shrine Shirube” – Utari Shrine Office === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === Related topics === * {{ill|lt=Xu Fu|Q700302|WD=Q700302|lt=Legend of Xu Fu|1=Xu Fu}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gosha Inari Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} === External links === {{commonscat|Utari-jinja}} * [https://www.toyokawa-map.net/spot/000291.html Utari Shrine] – Toyokawa City Tourism Association * [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=54zc-PluUGM Utari Shrine Wind Festival Tezutsu Fireworks [Toyokawa] [2013-04-13]] – YouTube {{Shinto sidebar}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Utari Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Utari Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mikawa Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:豊川市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mikawa Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:愛知県の重要文化財]] [[Category:豊川市の建築物]]

[[commons:Category:Utari-jinja]][[ja:菟足神社]] {{Infobox religious building | name = Utari Shrine | native_name = 菟足神社 | image = Le Temple Shintô Utari-jinja - Le haiden (La construction du culte).jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Utari Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.797491|137.36584}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }} {{nihongo|'''Utari Shrine'''|菟足神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. category|Category:Utari-jinja}} [[JA:菟足神社]] {{wikidata link|Q17216999}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Utari Shrine |Image = [[File:Le Temple Shintô Utari-jinja - Le haiden (La construction du culte).jpg|250px]]
Worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=230}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Toyokawa City|Q389495|WD=Q389495|1=Toyokawa, Aichi}} Kosakai-chō, Aza Miyawaki 2-1 |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|47|50.5|N|137|21|56.9|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Utari Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = Unakami Sukune no Mikoto |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (Minor)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |Founded = (Traditionally) {{ill|lt=Hakuhō period|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} year 15 ({{ill|lt=686|Q30619|WD=Q30619|1=686}}) |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage status = |Annual festival = Second {{ill|lt=Saturday|Q131|WD=Q131|lt=Sat|1=Saturday}} and {{ill|lt=Sunday|Q132|WD=Q132|1=Sunday}} of {{ill|lt=April|Q118|WD=Q118|1=April}} |Rituals = Wind Festival, Rice Paddy Festival |Map = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Utari Shrine|position=bottom|width=180|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Utari Shrine''' (うたりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Kosakai-chō, Aza Miyawaki, {{ill|lt=Toyokawa City|Q389495|WD=Q389495|1=Toyokawa, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was designated as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines the following single deity: * Unakami Sukune no Mikoto (うなかみすくねのみこと) === History === The enshrined deity, Unakami Sukune no Mikoto, is said to be a descendant of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōgen|Q333572|WD=Q333572|1=Emperor Kōgen}}, the great-great-grandchild (fourth generation) of {{ill|lt=Katsuragi Sotsuhiko no Mikoto|Q11620922|WD=Q11620922|1=Katsuragi Sotsuhiko}}. During the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235|WD=Q357235|1=Emperor Yūryaku}}, he was appointed as {{ill|lt=Hono no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11597049|WD=Q11597049|lt=provincial governor of Hono no Kuni (present-day Higashi-Mikawa)|1=provincial governor of Hono no Kuni (present-day Higashi-Mikawa)}}. Katsuragi Sotsuhiko no Mikoto was the father of {{ill|lt=Iwa no Hime no Mikoto|Q1066593|WD=Q1066593|1=Princess Iwa}}, the empress of {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}}, and is said to have wielded great power as a {{ill|lt=local ruling clan|Q84066|WD=Q84066|1=Gōzoku}} of Katsuragi in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}. After his death, Unakami Sukune no Mikoto was enshrined at Kashiwagi Beach in Hirai, {{ill|lt=Mikawa Province|Q768158|WD=Q768158|1=Mikawa Province}}, in recognition of his outstanding achievements in promoting industry and governing the people. It is said that during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}}, in the 15th year of the {{ill|lt=Hakuhō period|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} ({{ill|lt=686|Q30619|WD=Q30619|1=686}}), on {{ill|lt=April 11|Q2501|WD=Q2501|1=April 11}}, the shrine was relocated to its present site by Hata no Ishikatsu (はたのいしかつ). In the 6th year of the {{ill|lt=Jōgan era (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|1=Jōgan}} ({{ill|lt=864|Q25041|WD=Q25041|1=864}}), the shrine’s {{ill|lt=divine rank|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} was promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and its name appears in the Jinmyōchō of the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}. During the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, when the Emperor made his eastern progress, the shrine received an imperial envoy. In the 11th year of the {{ill|lt=Meiji era|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} ({{ill|lt=1878|Q7776|WD=Q7776|1=1878}}), it received a shrine name scroll in the imperial handwriting of {{ill|lt=Prince Arisugawa Taruhito|Q668338|WD=Q668338|1=Prince Arisugawa Taruhito}}. In the 7th year of the {{ill|lt=Taishō era|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} ({{ill|lt=1918|Q2094|WD=Q2094|1=1918}}), it was elevated from a village shrine to a prefectural shrine. === Festivals === * Wind Festival (Kazematsuri) – Held every year on the second Saturday and Sunday of April. {{ill|lt=Tezutsu fireworks|Q11497105|WD=Q11497105|1=Tezutsu-hanabi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=launched fireworks|1=UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill|lt=festival floats|Q11470974|WD=Q11470974|1=Matsuri float}} are dedicated. This festival is the origin of the name {{ill|lt=Kozakai Aoi Fūkan|Q108765371|WD=Q108765371|1=Kozakai Kifukan}}. * Rice Paddy Festival – Held on the 7th day of the 1st month of the lunar calendar. Important Cultural Properties (National designation) * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Daihannya Sutra|1=UNKNOWN}} (printed edition, 9 volumes) 585 volumes, sutras copied in a single hand by Ken’ichi from Angen 2 to Jishō 3 – Designated on {{ill|lt=June 30|Q2657|WD=Q2657|1=June 30}}, {{ill|lt=1961|Q3696|WD=Q3696|1=1961}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa era|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 36) ; Tangible Cultural Properties designated by Aichi Prefecture * {{ill|lt=Buddhist bell|Q8192099|WD=Q8192099|1=Bonshō}} – Designated on {{ill|lt=October 14|Q2922|WD=Q2922|1=October 14}}, {{ill|lt=1964|Q2652|WD=Q2652|1=1964}} (Shōwa 39) * Utari Shrine Festival Masks (5 masks) – Designated on {{ill|lt=May 21|Q2580|WD=Q2580|1=May 21}}, {{ill|lt=1965|Q2650|WD=Q2650|1=1965}} (Shōwa 40) ; Intangible Folk Cultural Property designated by Aichi Prefecture * Utari Shrine Rice Paddy Festival – {{ill|lt=1954|Q18581|WD=Q18581|1=1954}} (Shōwa 29), {{ill|lt=March 12|Q2402|WD=Q2402|1=March 12}} === On-site information === ;
